51:@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
52-class Quote:
53-    """Parsed quote snapshot."""
54-
55:    bid: float
56:    ask: float
57-    last: float
58-    spread: float
59-    change_percent: float
60-    high: float
61-    low: float
62-    volume: float
63-    updated: str
64-
65-    @classmethod
66:    def from_api(cls, raw: dict) -> Quote:
67:        bid = _val(raw.get("buy"), _val(raw.get("bid"), 0.0))
68:        ask = _val(raw.get("sell"), _val(raw.get("ask"), 0.0))
69:        last = _val(raw.get("last"), _val(raw.get("latest"), 0.0))
70:        spread = _val(raw.get("spread"))
71:        if spread is None:
72:            spread = round(ask - bid, 6) if (ask and bid) else 0.0
73:        change_percent = _val(raw.get("changePercent"), _val(raw.get("change_percent"), 0.0))
74:        high = _val(raw.get("highest"), _val(raw.get("high"), 0.0))
75:        low = _val(raw.get("lowest"), _val(raw.get("low"), 0.0))
76:        volume = _val(raw.get("totalVolumeTraded"), _val(raw.get("volume"), 0.0))
77:        updated = _ts(raw.get("updated", ""))
78-        return cls(
79:            bid=float(bid),
80:            ask=float(ask),
81-            last=float(last),
82-            spread=float(spread),
83-            change_percent=float(change_percent),
84-            high=float(high),
85-            low=float(low),
86-            volume=float(volume),
87-            updated=updated,
88-        )
89-

95-    price: float

98-    @classmethod

106:@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
107-class Trade:
108-    """A single executed trade from the market-data feed."""
109-
110-    price: float
111-    volume: int
112-    buyer: str
113-    seller: str
114-    time: str
115-
116-    @classmethod
117:    def from_api(cls, raw: dict) -> Trade:
118-        return cls(
119:            price=float(_val(raw.get("price"), 0.0)),
120:            volume=int(_val(raw.get("volume"), 0)),
121:            buyer=str(raw.get("buyer", "")),

124-        )

126-
127:@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
128-class MarketData:
129-    """Aggregated market data: quote + depth + recent trades."""
130-
131-    quote: Quote
132:    bid_levels: tuple[OrderDepthLevel, ...]
133:    ask_levels: tuple[OrderDepthLevel, ...]
134-    recent_trades: tuple[Trade, ...]
135-    market_maker_expected: bool
136-
137-    @classmethod
138:    def from_api(cls, raw: dict) -> MarketData:
139-        # Quote
140:        quote_raw = raw.get("quote", {})
141:        quote = Quote.from_api(quote_raw)
142-
143-        # Order depth
144:        depth = raw.get("orderDepth", {})
145-        levels = depth.get("levels", [])
146:        bid_levels: list[OrderDepthLevel] = []
147:        ask_levels: list[OrderDepthLevel] = []
148-        for lvl in levels:
149-            buy_side = lvl.get("buySide", {})
150-            sell_side = lvl.get("sellSide", {})
151-            if buy_side:
152:                bid_levels.append(OrderDepthLevel.from_api(buy_side))
153-            if sell_side:
154:                ask_levels.append(OrderDepthLevel.from_api(sell_side))
155-
156-        # Trades
157:        trades_raw = raw.get("trades", [])
158:        trades = tuple(Trade.from_api(t) for t in trades_raw)
159-
160-        mm = depth.get("marketMakerExpected", False)
161-
162-        return cls(
163-            quote=quote,
164:            bid_levels=tuple(bid_levels),
165:            ask_levels=tuple(ask_levels),
166-            recent_trades=trades,
167-            market_maker_expected=bool(mm),
168-        )

27:def get_positions(account_id: str | None = None) -> list[Position]:
28-    """Fetch all positions, optionally filtered to a single account.
29-
30-    Args:
31:        account_id: When provided only positions for this account are
32-            returned.  Otherwise all positions across all accounts.
33-
34-    Returns:
35-        List of :class:`~portfolio.avanza.types.Position`.
36-    """
37-    client = AvanzaClient.get_instance()
38-    raw: Any = client.get_positions_raw()
39-
40-    # The API may return a dict with a nested positions list, or a list.
41-    positions_raw: list[dict[str, Any]]
42-    if isinstance(raw, dict):
43-        # Prefer "withOrderbook" (newer API), fall back to "positions"
44-        positions_raw = raw.get("withOrderbook", raw.get("positions", []))
45-    elif isinstance(raw, list):
46-        positions_raw = raw
47-    else:
48-        positions_raw = []
49-
50-    positions = [Position.from_api(p) for p in positions_raw]
51-
52:    if account_id is not None:
53:        positions = [p for p in positions if p.account_id == str(account_id)]
54-
55-    logger.debug(
56:        "get_positions account_id=%s total=%d filtered=%d",
57:        account_id,
58-        len(positions_raw),
59-        len(positions),
60-    )
61-    return positions

38:def place_order(
39-    side: str,
40-    ob_id: str,
41-    price: float,
42-    volume: int,
43-    condition: str = "NORMAL",
44:    valid_until: str | None = None,

46-) -> OrderResult:
47-    """Place a BUY or SELL order.
48-
49-    Args:
50-        side: ``"BUY"`` or ``"SELL"``.
51-        ob_id: Avanza orderbook ID.
52-        price: Limit price.
53-        volume: Number of units.
54-        condition: Order condition (``"NORMAL"``, ``"FILL_OR_KILL"``,
55-            ``"FILL_AND_KILL"``).
56:        valid_until: ISO date string (default: today).
57:        account_id: Override default account.
58-
59-    Returns:
60-        :class:`~portfolio.avanza.types.OrderResult`.
61-
62-    Raises:
63-        ValueError: If volume < 1, price <= 0, or order total < 1000 SEK.
64-    """
65-    if volume < 1:
66-        raise ValueError(f"volume must be >= 1, got {volume}")
67-    if price <= 0:
68-        raise ValueError(f"price must be > 0, got {price}")
69-
70-    # 2026-04-17: match portfolio/avanza_session.py:590 convention — orders
71-    # below 1000 SEK pay the Avanza courtage minimum and are almost always
72-    # a caller bug. Unified-package callers should hit the same guard as
73-    # the legacy path.
74-    order_total = round(volume * price, 2)
75:    if order_total < 1000.0:
76-        raise ValueError(
77-            f"Order total {order_total:.2f} SEK below minimum 1000 SEK"
78-        )
79-

102-    return OrderResult.from_api(raw)

213:def place_stop_loss(
214-    ob_id: str,
215-    trigger_price: float,
216-    sell_price: float,

221:    account_id: str | None = None,
222-) -> StopLossResult:
223-    """Place a stop-loss order.
224-
225-    Args:
226-        ob_id: Avanza orderbook ID.
227-        trigger_price: Price that triggers the stop.
228-        sell_price: Limit price for the sell order when triggered.
229-        volume: Number of units to sell.
230-        valid_days: Days until the stop-loss expires (default 8).
231:        trigger_type: Trigger direction (``"LESS_OR_EQUAL"``,
232-            ``"FOLLOW_DOWNWARDS"``, etc.).
233:        value_type: Price type (``"MONETARY"`` or ``"PERCENTAGE"``).
234:        account_id: Override default account.
235-
236-    Returns:
237-        :class:`~portfolio.avanza.types.StopLossResult`.
238-    """
239-    client = AvanzaClient.get_instance()
240:    acct = account_id or client.account_id
241:    valid_until = date.today() + timedelta(days=valid_days)
242-
243-    # 2026-04-17: warn (don't raise) on sub-1000 SEK stop legs. Metals-loop
244-    # cascaded stops split a position into ≤3 legs; per-leg value may
245-    # legitimately fall below the courtage threshold. Surfacing via log
246-    # lets callers audit fee impact without breaking live cascading logic.
247:    if value_type == "MONETARY" and sell_price > 0:
248-        leg_total = round(volume * sell_price, 2)
249:        if leg_total < 1000.0:
250-            logger.warning(

252-                "(vol=%d sell=%.3f ob=%s)",
253-                leg_total, volume, sell_price, ob_id,
254-            )
255-
256-    trigger = StopLossTrigger(
257:        type=StopLossTriggerType(trigger_type),
258-        value=trigger_price,
259:        valid_until=valid_until,
260:        value_type=StopLossPriceType(value_type),
261-    )
262-
263-    order_event = StopLossOrderEvent(
264-        type=OrderType.SELL,
265-        price=sell_price,
266-        volume=volume,
267-        valid_days=valid_days,
268:        price_type=StopLossPriceType(value_type),
269-        short_selling_allowed=False,
270-    )
271-

277-        order_event,
278-    )
279-

346-    """Delete a stop-loss order.  Idempotent — 404 is treated as success.

350-    """

355-        logger.info("delete_stop_loss stop_id=%s -> OK", stop_id)
356-        return True
357-    except Exception as exc:
358-        # 404 means already deleted — treat as success
359-        exc_str = str(exc).lower()
360-        if "404" in exc_str or "not found" in exc_str:
361-            logger.info("delete_stop_loss stop_id=%s -> already gone (404)", stop_id)
362-            return True
363-        logger.error("delete_stop_loss stop_id=%s -> FAILED: %s", stop_id, exc)
364-        return False

"C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe" -NoProfile -Command "rg -n --color never -C 20 \"DISALLOWED_CATEGORY_FRAGMENTS|SKIP_ENV_VAR|_walk_accounts|_category_disallowed|verify_default_account|raise_on_mismatch|use_cache|result = \\{|warrants can trade here|disallowed_category|not found\" Q:\\finance-analyzer\\portfolio\\avanza_account_check.py" in Q:\finance-analyzer
 succeeded in 660ms:
3-User reported on 2026-05-11 that the dashboard's account view for
4-account 1625505 (the hard-coded ``DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`` in
5-``portfolio/avanza_session.py``) showed Beammwave / NextEra / Vertiv
6-holdings — typical ISK long-term holdings, not the warrant-trading
7-positions the bots are supposed to manage. If the configured account is
8-an ISK, every live order has been routing to the wrong place.
9-
10-This module fetches the categorized-accounts overview at startup,
11-finds the configured account, and inspects its category. If the
12-category matches an ISK / pension / insurance pattern (where leveraged
13-warrants cannot legally trade in Sweden anyway), it logs a critical
14-error, emits a Telegram alert, and raises ``AccountCategoryMismatch``
15-so the caller can fail closed before the first order placement.
16-
17-Override knob: setting the env var ``PF_SKIP_ACCOUNT_CHECK=1`` makes
18-the verification log-and-continue instead of raising. Use during a
19-known-bad window only — the warning still lands in
20-``data/critical_errors.jsonl`` so the issue stays surfaced.
21-
22-Result is cached for the lifetime of the process so the metals loop,
23:golddigger, and grid fisher can each call ``verify_default_account()``
24-during their respective inits without re-hammering the API.
25-"""
26-
27-from __future__ import annotations
28-
29-import datetime as _dt
30-import logging
31-import os
32-import threading
33-from typing import Optional
34-
35-from portfolio.file_utils import atomic_append_jsonl
36-
37-logger = logging.getLogger("portfolio.avanza_account_check")
38-
39-# Category strings that disqualify an account from leveraged-warrant
40-# trading. Empty by default — 2026-05-11 user confirmation made it
41-# clear that the original premise ("ISK forbids warrants") was wrong:
42-# Swedish ISK accounts legally hold warrants, certificates, and ETPs;
43-# the tax treatment differs but the trades are routine. Same for
44-# Kapitalförsäkring (insurance wrapper) and Tjänstepension — those
45-# can trade leveraged instruments too. The verifier now confirms the
46-# account *exists* under the configured ID and logs its category; the
47-# operator owns the decision about what to trade where.
48-#
49-# If a future category genuinely cannot trade warrants (e.g. a
50-# restricted minor account), add the fragment here. Substring match
51-# is case-insensitive.
52:DISALLOWED_CATEGORY_FRAGMENTS: tuple[str, ...] = ()
53-
54-
55-CRITICAL_ERRORS_LOG = "data/critical_errors.jsonl"
56:SKIP_ENV_VAR = "PF_SKIP_ACCOUNT_CHECK"
57-
58-_cache_lock = threading.Lock()
59-_cache_result: Optional[dict] = None
60-
61-
62-class AccountCategoryMismatch(RuntimeError):
63-    """Raised when the configured DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID lands on a
64-    disqualified category (ISK / pension / insurance)."""
65-
66-
67-def _now_iso() -> str:
68-    return _dt.datetime.now(_dt.timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
69-
70-
71:def _walk_accounts(data: dict):
72-    """Yield ``(category_label, account_dict)`` for every account in
73-    every known response shape. Mirrors the multi-path walk that
74-    ``avanza_session.get_buying_power`` uses so we cover shape drift.
75-    """
76-    if not isinstance(data, dict):
77-        return
78-    # Path A: legacy categorizedAccounts[].accounts[]
79-    for cat in (data.get("categorizedAccounts") or []):
80-        label = cat.get("name") or cat.get("type") or cat.get("category") or ""
81-        for acc in (cat.get("accounts") or []):
82-            yield str(label), acc
83-    # Path B: new flat shape — accounts[]. Each account carries its
84-    # own category field.
85-    for acc in (data.get("accounts") or []):
86-        label = (acc.get("type") or acc.get("category")
87-                 or acc.get("accountType") or "")
88-        yield str(label), acc
89-    # Path C: new categorized shape — categories[].accounts[]
90-    for cat in (data.get("categories") or []):
91-        label = (cat.get("name") or cat.get("type")
92-                 or cat.get("category") or "")
93-        for acc in (cat.get("accounts") or []):
94-            yield str(label), acc
95-
96-
97-def _extract_account_id(acc: dict) -> Optional[str]:
98-    """Pick whichever ID field this account exposes. Same precedence
99-    order as ``avanza_session.get_buying_power``."""
100-    if not isinstance(acc, dict):
101-        return None
102-    for key in ("accountId", "id", "accountNumber", "number"):
103-        val = acc.get(key)
104-        if val is not None:
105-            return str(val)
106-    return None
107-
108-
109:def _category_disallowed(label: str) -> bool:
110-    """Match the category label against the disqualifying fragments
111-    case-insensitively. Empty label → not disqualified (caller logs
112-    a warning instead)."""
113-    if not label:
114-        return False
115-    norm = label.lower()
116:    return any(fragment in norm for fragment in DISALLOWED_CATEGORY_FRAGMENTS)
117-
118-
119-def _record_critical_error(account_id: str, label: str, reason: str) -> None:
120-    entry = {
121-        "ts": _now_iso(),
122-        "level": "critical",
123-        "category": "avanza_account_mismatch",
124-        "caller": "portfolio.avanza_account_check",
125-        "message": (
126-            f"DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID={account_id} category={label!r} "
127-            f"reason={reason}"
128-        ),
129-        "context": {
130-            "account_id": account_id,
131-            "category_label": label,
132-            "reason": reason,
133-        },
134-    }
135-    try:
136-        atomic_append_jsonl(CRITICAL_ERRORS_LOG, entry)
--
160-    has an LLM worker + Playwright swing-trader page sharing the main
161-    thread's event loop) don't trip ``Playwright Sync API inside the
162-    asyncio loop``. Without the worker, every verifier invocation
163-    inside metals_loop returned ``fetch_failed`` — the helper would
164-    never actually catch an ISK mismatch in production. Same pattern
165-    ``GridFisher._safe_session_call`` uses.
166-    """
167-    import concurrent.futures  # noqa: PLC0415
168-
169-    from portfolio.avanza_session import api_get  # noqa: PLC0415
170-
171-    def _runner():
172-        return api_get("/_api/account-overview/overview/categorizedAccounts")
173-
174-    with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(
175-        max_workers=1, thread_name_prefix="avanza-acct-check",
176-    ) as pool:
177-        return pool.submit(_runner).result(timeout=30)
178-
179-
180:def verify_default_account(
181-    account_id: Optional[str] = None,
182-    *,
183:    raise_on_mismatch: bool = True,
184:    use_cache: bool = True,
185-) -> dict:
186-    """Confirm the configured Avanza account is a trading-class account.
187-
188-    Returns a dict describing the verification result:
189-      ``{"ok": bool, "account_id": str, "category": str, "reason": str}``
190-
191:    When ``ok`` is ``False`` and ``raise_on_mismatch`` is True, raises
192-    :class:`AccountCategoryMismatch`. The ``PF_SKIP_ACCOUNT_CHECK=1``
193:    env var overrides ``raise_on_mismatch`` to ``False`` so an
194-    operator can keep the loops running while they investigate.
195-
196-    Args:
197-        account_id: Account ID to verify. Defaults to
198-            ``avanza_session.DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`` so most callers can
199:            simply call ``verify_default_account()``.
200:        raise_on_mismatch: When True, raise on disallowed category.
201-            Setting ``PF_SKIP_ACCOUNT_CHECK=1`` env always wins and
202-            forces ``False``.
203:        use_cache: When True (default), reuse a previous successful
204-            verification for the same account ID within this process.
205-            Failed verifications are NOT cached — they re-check on each
206-            call so a fixed config picks up immediately.
207-    """
208-    global _cache_result
209-
210-    if account_id is None:
211-        from portfolio.avanza_session import DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID  # noqa: PLC0415
212-        account_id = DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID
213-    account_id = str(account_id)
214-
215-    with _cache_lock:
216:        if (use_cache and _cache_result
217-                and _cache_result.get("account_id") == account_id
218-                and _cache_result.get("ok")):
219-            return dict(_cache_result)
220-
221:    skip_raise = os.environ.get(SKIP_ENV_VAR, "").strip() in ("1", "true", "yes")
222:    effective_raise = raise_on_mismatch and not skip_raise
223-
224-    try:
225-        data = _api_get_categorized_accounts()
226-    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
227-        # Codex P2 fix (2026-05-11): a transient categorizedAccounts
228-        # outage (DNS flap, 5xx, auth blip) must NOT permanently brick
229-        # the grid fisher for the rest of the process. Only positive
230:        # mismatches (disallowed_category / account_not_found) fail
231-        # closed. fetch_failed downgrades to a logged warning + a
232-        # critical_errors entry so the operator can still see the
233-        # outage. Callers can retry verification before the first
234-        # order placement; if Avanza is genuinely unreachable, the
235-        # actual order placement will fail too and the order-side
236-        # guards still apply.
237:        result = {
238-            "ok": False,
239-            "account_id": account_id,
240-            "category": "",
241-            "reason": f"fetch_failed:{exc!s}",
242-        }
243-        logger.warning(
244:            "verify_default_account: fetch failed account=%s err=%r — "
245-            "downgraded to warning, NOT raising (transient outage path)",
246-            account_id, exc,
247-        )
248-        _record_critical_error(account_id, "", result["reason"])
249-        return result
250-
251-    found_label: Optional[str] = None
252-    seen_ids: list[str] = []
253:    for label, acc in _walk_accounts(data):
254-        acc_id = _extract_account_id(acc)
255-        if acc_id is not None:
256-            seen_ids.append(acc_id)
257-        if acc_id == account_id:
258-            found_label = label
259-            break
260-
261-    if found_label is None:
262:        result = {
263-            "ok": False,
264-            "account_id": account_id,
265-            "category": "",
266-            "reason": "account_not_found",
267-            "seen_ids": seen_ids,
268-        }
269-        logger.warning(
270:            "verify_default_account: %s not in categorizedAccounts. "
271-            "seen=%s", account_id, seen_ids,
272-        )
273-        _record_critical_error(account_id, "", "account_not_found")
274-        _send_telegram(
275:            f"⚠️ Avanza account {account_id} not found in "
276-            f"categorizedAccounts. Saw: {seen_ids[:8]}"
277-        )
278-        if effective_raise:
279-            raise AccountCategoryMismatch(
280-                f"Account {account_id} not present in Avanza "
281-                f"categorizedAccounts response (saw {seen_ids})"
282-            )
283-        return result
284-
285:    if _category_disallowed(found_label):
286:        result = {
287-            "ok": False,
288-            "account_id": account_id,
289-            "category": found_label,
290:            "reason": "disallowed_category",
291-        }
292-        logger.error(
293:            "verify_default_account: %s lives in disallowed category %r — "
294-            "warrants cannot trade here", account_id, found_label,
295-        )
296:        _record_critical_error(account_id, found_label, "disallowed_category")
297-        _send_telegram(
298-            f"🚨 Avanza account {account_id} category {found_label!r} "
299-            f"disallows warrant trading. Update DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID."
300-        )
301-        if effective_raise:
302-            raise AccountCategoryMismatch(
303-                f"Account {account_id} category {found_label!r} disallows "
304-                f"warrant trading"
305-            )
306-        return result
307-
308:    result = {
309-        "ok": True,
310-        "account_id": account_id,
311-        "category": found_label or "<empty>",
312-        "reason": "trading_class",
313-    }
314-    logger.info(
315:        "verify_default_account: %s category=%s OK",
316-        account_id, found_label or "<empty>",
317-    )
318-    with _cache_lock:
319-        _cache_result = dict(result)
320-    return result
321-
322-
323-def reset_cache() -> None:
324-    """Drop the cached verification — useful in tests."""
325-    global _cache_result
326-    with _cache_lock:
327-        _cache_result = None

28:def get_tick_rules(ob_id: str) -> list[TickEntry]:

33-

40-

48-    _cache[ob_id] = entries

50-    return entries

53:def round_to_tick(price: float, ob_id: str, direction: str = "down") -> float:

57-    floating-point drift.
58-
59-    Args:
60-        price: The price to round.
61-        ob_id: Avanza orderbook ID (needed to fetch the tick table).
62-        direction: ``"down"`` (floor) or ``"up"`` (ceil).
63-
--
109-# Internals
110-# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
111-
112-
113-def _find_tick_for_price(price: float, entries: list[TickEntry]) -> float | None:
114-    """Find the tick size applicable for *price*.
115-
116-    Returns ``None`` if no entry matches.
117-    """
118-    for entry in entries:
119:        if entry.min_price <= price <= entry.max_price:
120:            return entry.tick_size
121:        # Handle unbounded upper range (max_price == 0 means infinity)
122:        if entry.min_price <= price and entry.max_price == 0:
123:            return entry.tick_size
124-    # Fallback: if price exceeds all ranges, use the last entry
125-    if entries:
126:        return entries[-1].tick_size
127-    return None
128-
129-
130-def _decimal_places(value: float) -> int:
131-    """Count the number of significant decimal places in *value*."""
132-    s = f"{value:.10f}".rstrip("0")
133-    if "." in s:
134-        return len(s.split(".")[1])
135-    return 0

53:def round_to_tick(price: float, ob_id: str, direction: str = "down") -> float:
54-    """Round a price to the nearest valid tick.
55-
56-    Uses integer arithmetic (multiply -> floor/ceil -> divide) to avoid
57-    floating-point drift.
58-
59-    Args:
60-        price: The price to round.
61-        ob_id: Avanza orderbook ID (needed to fetch the tick table).
62-        direction: ``"down"`` (floor) or ``"up"`` (ceil).
63-
64-    Returns:
65:        The rounded price.
66-
67-    Raises:
68-        ValueError: If *direction* is not ``"down"`` or ``"up"``.
69-        ValueError: If no tick rule matches *price*.
70-    """
71:    if direction not in ("down", "up"):
72-        raise ValueError(f"direction must be 'down' or 'up', got {direction!r}")
73-
74-    entries = get_tick_rules(ob_id)
75-    tick = _find_tick_for_price(price, entries)
76-
77-    if tick is None:
78-        raise ValueError(f"No tick rule found for price {price} (ob_id={ob_id})")
79-
80-    # Integer arithmetic to avoid float drift:
81-    # steps = price / tick  ->  round to int  ->  result = steps * tick
82-    # We use a precision multiplier derived from the tick's decimal places.
83-    precision = _decimal_places(tick)
84-    multiplier = 10 ** precision
85-
86-    # Convert to integer domain
87-    price_int = price * multiplier
88-    tick_int = round(tick * multiplier)
89-
90-    if tick_int == 0:
91-        return price  # degenerate tick; return unchanged
92-
93-    if direction == "down":
94-        steps = math.floor(price_int / tick_int)
95-    else:
96-        steps = math.ceil(price_int / tick_int)
97-
98-    result = (steps * tick_int) / multiplier
99:    return round(result, precision)
100-
101-
102-def clear_cache() -> None:
103-    """Clear the module-level tick-rule cache."""
104-    _cache.clear()
105-    logger.debug("tick_rules cache cleared")

159-def delete_order_live(page, account_id: str | None, order_id: str):
160-    """Cancel an open order via the authenticated page session.
161-
162-    IMPORTANT: Uses POST to /_api/trading-critical/rest/order/delete with
163-    JSON body {accountId, orderId}. The DELETE HTTP verb to
164:    /_api/trading-critical/rest/order/{accountId}/{orderId} returns 404
165-    (Avanza API change discovered 2026-03-24).
166-    """
167-    csrf = get_csrf(page)
168-    if not csrf:
169:        return False, {"error": "no CSRF token"}
170-
171-    resolved_account_id = str(account_id or get_account_id())
172-    try:
173-        # 2026-04-13: cross-process order lock (see metals_avanza_helpers.place_order).
174-        with avanza_order_lock(op=f"delete_order_live/{order_id}"):
175-            result = page.evaluate(
176-                """async (args) => {
177-                    const [accountId, orderId, token] = args;
178-                    const resp = await fetch(
179-                        'https://www.avanza.se/_api/trading-critical/rest/order/delete',
180-                        {
181-                            method: 'POST',
182-                            headers: {
183-                                'Content-Type': 'application/json',
184-                                'X-SecurityToken': token,
185-                            },
186-                            credentials: 'include',
187-                            body: JSON.stringify({accountId: accountId, orderId: orderId}),
188-                        }
189-                    );
190-                    return {status: resp.status, body: await resp.text()};
191-                }""",
192-                [resolved_account_id, order_id, csrf],
193-            )
194:        http_status = int(result.get("status") or 0)
195-        body_text = result.get("body", "")
196-        parsed = {}
197-        try:
198-            if body_text:
199-                parsed = json.loads(body_text)
200-        except (TypeError, json.JSONDecodeError):
201-            parsed = {}
202-        success = parsed.get("orderRequestStatus") == "SUCCESS"
203-        return success, {
204:            "http_status": http_status,
205-            "parsed": parsed,
206-            "body": body_text,
207-        }
208-    except Exception as exc:
209-        logger.error("Delete order failed for order %s: %s", order_id, exc, exc_info=True)
210:        return False, {"error": str(exc)}

225-                    const [accountId, stopId, token] = args;

237-            )
238:        http_status = int(result.get("status") or 0)
239:        # 2xx = deleted successfully.  404 = stop already gone (triggered/expired/cancelled).

242-        body_text = result.get("body", "")

244-        try:
245-            if body_text:

248-            parsed = {}
249-        return success, {
250:            "http_status": http_status,
251-            "parsed": parsed,
252-            "body": body_text,
253-        }
254-    except Exception as exc:
255-        logger.error("Delete stop-loss failed for stop %s: %s", stop_id, exc, exc_info=True)

1-"""Avanza order confirmation flow — human-in-the-loop for real money.
2-
3-Workflow:
4-1. Layer 2 calls request_order() → saves intent to pending orders, returns details
5:   (including a unique 6-hex `confirm_token`).
6-2. Layer 2 sends Telegram message with order details + "Reply CONFIRM <token>
7-   to execute".
8-3. Main loop calls check_pending_orders() each cycle.
9-4. On CONFIRM <token> reply → execute the order whose token matches, notify
10-   via Telegram.
11-5. On timeout (5 min) → expire the pending order, notify.
12-
13:P1-10 (2026-05-02): per-order `confirm_token` eliminates three races the
14-old bare-CONFIRM design suffered from (see test class docstrings):
15-- stale-CONFIRM race (replayed CONFIRM confirms a NEWER order)
16-- wrong-order race (sort-by-time-DESC matches the wrong order)
17-- no-pending-yet race (CONFIRM lands before the order it was for)
18-
19-Bare CONFIRM (no token) is still accepted but ONLY matches LEGACY orders
20:that have no `confirm_token` field — i.e. orders that were already in
21-flight when this code was deployed. New orders MUST be confirmed by token.
22-"""
23-
24-import contextlib
25-import logging
26-import re
27-import secrets
28-import uuid
--
54-# CONFIRM prefix matcher. Word boundary required because "confirmed" /
55-# "confirms" / "confirmation" parse to "confirm" + a hex-valid suffix
56-# ("ed", "s", "ation") which would silently match against legacy orders
57-# or non-existent tokens. Anchored at start since the user is asked to
58-# reply with "CONFIRM <token>" as the entire message.
59-_CONFIRM_PREFIX_RE = re.compile(r"^confirm(?:\s+|$)")
60-
61-
62:def _generate_confirm_token() -> str:
63-    """Return a fresh hex token for a new pending order. Module-level
64-    indirection keeps tests deterministic via patch.object if ever needed."""
65-    return secrets.token_hex(_CONFIRM_TOKEN_HEX_CHARS // 2)
66-

135-    # Log the token at INFO so an operator reading agent.log can read it

139:    logger.info(

142-    )

151-def check_pending_orders(config: dict) -> list[dict]:
152-    """Check for Telegram confirmations and expire stale orders.
153-
154-    Called by the main loop each cycle. Polls Telegram getUpdates for
155-    CONFIRM <token> replies. Executes confirmed orders (matched by token)
156-    and expires timed-out ones.
157-
158-    P1-10 (2026-05-02): a CONFIRM <token> reply confirms ONLY the order
159:    whose ``confirm_token`` matches. Bare CONFIRM (no token) still works
160-    but ONLY matches LEGACY orders without a token field — so freshly
161-    created orders cannot be silently confirmed by a stale CONFIRM that
162-    was replayed by a getUpdates offset bug.
163-
164-    Args:
165-        config: App config dict (with telegram.token, telegram.chat_id,
166:            and optionally telegram.allowed_user_id for sender auth).
167-
168-    Returns:
169-        List of orders that were acted on (confirmed or expired) this cycle.
170-    """
171-    pending = _load_pending()
172-    if not pending:
173-        return []
174-
--
179-    # string) — only matches legacy orders without a token.
180-    confirmed_tokens = _check_telegram_confirm(config)
181-
182-    for order in pending:
183-        if order["status"] != "pending_confirmation":
184-            continue
185-
186-        expires = datetime.fromisoformat(order["expires"])
187:        order_token = order.get("confirm_token", "")
188-
189-        # P1-10: matching rules.
190-        # 1. Order has a token AND that token is in confirmed_tokens → confirm.
191-        # 2. Order has NO token (legacy in-flight order) AND bare CONFIRM
192-        #    arrived ("" in the set) → confirm. This is the backwards-compat
193-        #    path for orders that existed before the deploy.
194-        # 3. Otherwise → no confirmation this cycle (may still expire).
195-        confirmed_by_token = bool(order_token) and order_token in confirmed_tokens

218-def _check_telegram_confirm(config: dict) -> set[str]:
219-    """Poll Telegram for CONFIRM <token> replies from the configured chat.
220-
221-    Returns ``set[str]`` of matched tokens (lowercase hex). Bare CONFIRM
222-    (with no token) is represented as ``""`` and matches only LEGACY
223:    pending orders without a ``confirm_token`` field. Anything that's not
224-    valid hex after CONFIRM (e.g. ``CONFIRM xyz`` typo) is silently
225-    dropped — never matched against an order — so a typo doesn't
226-    accidentally confirm via the legacy path.
227-
228-    Uses getUpdates with a stored offset to avoid reprocessing old messages.
229-
230-    AV-P1-3 (2026-05-02): Sender-authenticated when
231:    ``telegram.allowed_user_id`` is set. Without sender auth, the chat-only
232-    filter is bypassable in two ways:
233-      - Group chats: anyone admitted can send CONFIRM and execute the
234-        pending order.
235-      - Bot-token compromise: an attacker who has the bot token can
236-        deliver fake updates with the right ``chat_id`` and execute orders.
237:    When ``allowed_user_id`` is unset the chat-only check is preserved
238-    (backwards-compatible). The offset still advances on dropped messages
239-    so we don't re-process the rejected update every cycle.

# Codex adversarial review: avanza-api
## Summary
Two blockers stand out: the newer `portfolio/avanza/*` stack can still route live orders to an arbitrary configured account, and `delete_stop_loss_no_page()` reports broker-side delete failures as success. I did not find Playwright async-API usage or a regression back to the regular order endpoint for stop-loss placement in the reviewed files.

## P0 — Blockers
- `portfolio/avanza/client.py:65-70` stores `avanza.account_id` from config with no whitelist/category guard, and the unified trading path blindly uses it for live mutations in `portfolio/avanza/trading.py:81-88`, `129-137`, `160-165`, `240-278`, `352-355`. The hardened guard in `portfolio/avanza_session.py:35-43` and `590-592` is never applied here. A config typo or stale pension/KF account id will place, modify, cancel, or stop-loss real orders on the wrong account.
- `portfolio/avanza_control.py:395-404` treats any non-exception result from `_api_delete()` as success unless the body happens to contain `errorCode`. But `_api_delete()`/`api_delete()` returns only `{"http_status": ..., "ok": ...}` on DELETE failures (`portfolio/avanza_session.py:347-376`). A `401`, `403`, or `500` stop-loss delete is therefore reported as cleared, which can let downstream sell logic proceed while the stop still encumbers volume.

## P1 — High
- `portfolio/avanza_client.py:172-178` switches `get_positions()` to the BankID session path whenever session auth is available, and `portfolio/avanza_session.py:682-716` appends every position from `withOrderbook` without filtering by `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID` or `ALLOWED_ACCOUNT_IDS`. The TOTP fallback does filter (`portfolio/avanza_client.py:183-199`). Once BankID is live, ignored pension/KF holdings can leak back into the trading view.
- Tick enforcement exists but is unused. `portfolio/avanza/tick_rules.py:53-99` defines `round_to_tick()`, yet raw prices are sent straight to Avanza in `portfolio/avanza_session.py:583-621` and `749-801`, `portfolio/avanza_client.py:345-367`, and `portfolio/avanza/trading.py:65-102` and `239-288`. The same paths only check `volume < 1` and never normalize non-integer `volume`. That leaves legal-tick and integer-volume enforcement entirely to callers, which violates the subsystem invariant.
- `portfolio/avanza/streaming.py:118-137` only backs off inside the exception path. `portfolio/avanza/streaming.py:208-214` handles `WebSocketConnectionClosedException` by returning normally from `_read_loop()`, so the outer loop reconnects immediately with no sleep. Under transient socket closes you get a tight reconnect loop instead of bounded backoff.
- The account-category guard has drifted into a no-op. `portfolio/avanza_account_check.py:39-52` sets `DISALLOWED_CATEGORY_FRAGMENTS = ()`, and `verify_default_account()` only rejects when `_category_disallowed()` is true (`109-116`, `285-306`). That means any existing account category now passes as long as the ID exists, which no longer matches the stated allowed set `(INVESTERINGSSPARKONTO, KAPITALFORSAKRING, PENSION)`.

## P2 — Medium
- The BankID scanner fallback ignores the requested instrument type. In `portfolio/avanza/scanner.py:77-78`, `_search(itype_str, query, limit)` posts only `{"query": query, "limit": limit}` to `/_api/search/filtered-search`; `itype_str` is unused. `scan_instruments()` then loops separately for `"certificate"` and `"warrant"` (`165-177`) but both searches can return the same mixed result set, which is later truncated by `unique_hits[:max_search]` (`195-201`).
- `portfolio/avanza/types.py:364-371` parses `SearchHit.orderbook_id` from `id` or `orderbookId` only. The same subsystem expects `orderBookId` elsewhere (`portfolio/avanza/scanner.py:197` and `207`). When search responses use `orderBookId`, typed `search()`/`find_warrants()` results come back with blank `orderbook_id`, recreating the `orderbook_id`/identifier confusion you called out.
- `portfolio/avanza_session.py:31-32` declares a 30-minute session expiry buffer, but `load_session()` only rejects sessions when `exp <= now` (`83-95`). New requests are still allowed in the last 30 minutes of the manual BankID window, so expiry is handled reactively by live request failures instead of proactively at session load.

## P3 — Low
- `portfolio/avanza_orders.py:135-141` logs each live `confirm_token` at INFO. It is not an Avanza session cookie, but it is still a short-lived order-authorization secret and should not be written to plain logs.

## Tests missing
- A regression test that `delete_stop_loss_no_page()` returns failure when `_api_delete()` yields `{"http_status": 401/403/500, "ok": false}` and success only on `2xx`/`404`.
- An integration test that the unified `portfolio/avanza/*` trading path rejects any account outside the allowed policy, and that `get_positions()` never surfaces pension/KF holdings when BankID session auth is active.
- Placement tests that every order and stop-loss path rounds price via `round_to_tick()` and rejects/normalizes non-integer volume before submission.
- A streaming test where the server closes the socket cleanly and the client proves it sleeps/backoffs before reconnecting.
- A scanner/search test for the BankID fallback that asserts instrument-type filtering is honored and that `SearchHit.from_api()` correctly parses `orderBookId`.
- An account-check test that only the allowed category set passes and that other categories fail closed.
tokens used
231,774
# Codex adversarial review: avanza-api
## Summary
Two blockers stand out: the newer `portfolio/avanza/*` stack can still route live orders to an arbitrary configured account, and `delete_stop_loss_no_page()` reports broker-side delete failures as success. I did not find Playwright async-API usage or a regression back to the regular order endpoint for stop-loss placement in the reviewed files.

## P0 — Blockers
- `portfolio/avanza/client.py:65-70` stores `avanza.account_id` from config with no whitelist/category guard, and the unified trading path blindly uses it for live mutations in `portfolio/avanza/trading.py:81-88`, `129-137`, `160-165`, `240-278`, `352-355`. The hardened guard in `portfolio/avanza_session.py:35-43` and `590-592` is never applied here. A config typo or stale pension/KF account id will place, modify, cancel, or stop-loss real orders on the wrong account.
- `portfolio/avanza_control.py:395-404` treats any non-exception result from `_api_delete()` as success unless the body happens to contain `errorCode`. But `_api_delete()`/`api_delete()` returns only `{"http_status": ..., "ok": ...}` on DELETE failures (`portfolio/avanza_session.py:347-376`). A `401`, `403`, or `500` stop-loss delete is therefore reported as cleared, which can let downstream sell logic proceed while the stop still encumbers volume.

## P1 — High
- `portfolio/avanza_client.py:172-178` switches `get_positions()` to the BankID session path whenever session auth is available, and `portfolio/avanza_session.py:682-716` appends every position from `withOrderbook` without filtering by `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID` or `ALLOWED_ACCOUNT_IDS`. The TOTP fallback does filter (`portfolio/avanza_client.py:183-199`). Once BankID is live, ignored pension/KF holdings can leak back into the trading view.
- Tick enforcement exists but is unused. `portfolio/avanza/tick_rules.py:53-99` defines `round_to_tick()`, yet raw prices are sent straight to Avanza in `portfolio/avanza_session.py:583-621` and `749-801`, `portfolio/avanza_client.py:345-367`, and `portfolio/avanza/trading.py:65-102` and `239-288`. The same paths only check `volume < 1` and never normalize non-integer `volume`. That leaves legal-tick and integer-volume enforcement entirely to callers, which violates the subsystem invariant.
- `portfolio/avanza/streaming.py:118-137` only backs off inside the exception path. `portfolio/avanza/streaming.py:208-214` handles `WebSocketConnectionClosedException` by returning normally from `_read_loop()`, so the outer loop reconnects immediately with no sleep. Under transient socket closes you get a tight reconnect loop instead of bounded backoff.
- The account-category guard has drifted into a no-op. `portfolio/avanza_account_check.py:39-52` sets `DISALLOWED_CATEGORY_FRAGMENTS = ()`, and `verify_default_account()` only rejects when `_category_disallowed()` is true (`109-116`, `285-306`). That means any existing account category now passes as long as the ID exists, which no longer matches the stated allowed set `(INVESTERINGSSPARKONTO, KAPITALFORSAKRING, PENSION)`.

## P2 — Medium
- The BankID scanner fallback ignores the requested instrument type. In `portfolio/avanza/scanner.py:77-78`, `_search(itype_str, query, limit)` posts only `{"query": query, "limit": limit}` to `/_api/search/filtered-search`; `itype_str` is unused. `scan_instruments()` then loops separately for `"certificate"` and `"warrant"` (`165-177`) but both searches can return the same mixed result set, which is later truncated by `unique_hits[:max_search]` (`195-201`).
- `portfolio/avanza/types.py:364-371` parses `SearchHit.orderbook_id` from `id` or `orderbookId` only. The same subsystem expects `orderBookId` elsewhere (`portfolio/avanza/scanner.py:197` and `207`). When search responses use `orderBookId`, typed `search()`/`find_warrants()` results come back with blank `orderbook_id`, recreating the `orderbook_id`/identifier confusion you called out.
- `portfolio/avanza_session.py:31-32` declares a 30-minute session expiry buffer, but `load_session()` only rejects sessions when `exp <= now` (`83-95`). New requests are still allowed in the last 30 minutes of the manual BankID window, so expiry is handled reactively by live request failures instead of proactively at session load.

## P3 — Low
- `portfolio/avanza_orders.py:135-141` logs each live `confirm_token` at INFO. It is not an Avanza session cookie, but it is still a short-lived order-authorization secret and should not be written to plain logs.

## Tests missing
- A regression test that `delete_stop_loss_no_page()` returns failure when `_api_delete()` yields `{"http_status": 401/403/500, "ok": false}` and success only on `2xx`/`404`.
- An integration test that the unified `portfolio/avanza/*` trading path rejects any account outside the allowed policy, and that `get_positions()` never surfaces pension/KF holdings when BankID session auth is active.
- Placement tests that every order and stop-loss path rounds price via `round_to_tick()` and rejects/normalizes non-integer volume before submission.
- A streaming test where the server closes the socket cleanly and the client proves it sleeps/backoffs before reconnecting.
